query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed the research and delivered both the full markdown synthesis and structured findings. Here's a concise recap of the key findings:

**Bottom line:** Hichilema (UPND) enters the 13 August 2026 election as a strong favorite, but the race is more competitive than the headline poll suggests.

**Polling:**
- **October 2025 Mutombo/UNZA poll** (n=1,009, ±3.1%): HH **60%** (58–63% range) vs. **~35%** combined opposition — the headline figure you referenced.
- Unscientific March 2026 online poll: HH ~52%, Mundubile ~27%.
- Academic ZERN/IDCPPA survey (March 2026) exists but detailed vote numbers weren't publicly accessible.

**Opposition/Lungu:**
- **Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025** in Pretoria — he was already barred from running (Dec 2024 ConCourt ruling). His death fractured the PF and spawned a still-unresolved burial dispute.
- The opposition has partly consolidated behind **Brian Mundubile** (Tonse–PF "Pamodzi" Alliance, running mate Makebi Zulu), who has shown surprising momentum on ex-PF networks. Fred M'membe (Socialist Party) runs separately after the broader united-opposition effort collapsed.

**Alliance-driven framing:** Confirmed — **Constitution Amendment Act No. 7 of 2025 ("Bill 7," enacted Dec 2025)** expanded parliament and reserved seats, plus a delimitation exercise critics call gerrymandering. With 14 approved candidates and a 50%+1 threshold, opposition fragmentation strongly favors HH.

**Turnout:** ~8.7M registered voters; high turnout expected; youth bloc decisive (as in 2021). Economic grievances (cost of living, jobs, electricity) are the opposition's main opening.

**Key caveat:** Zambian polling is thin and historically unreliable, so figures are directional rather than precise.

key_facts: Zambia's general elections are scheduled for 13 August 2026 (president, National Assembly, councillors, council chairs).

October 2025 Mutombo/UNZA national poll (n=1,009, all 10 provinces, ±3.1%, fielded 2–15 Oct 2025): Hichilema 60% (range 58–63%) vs ~35% combined opposition; a related report cited a 75% approval rating.
Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025 in Pretoria, South Africa; he was already barred from running by a December 2024 Constitutional Court ruling on his term limit.
Lungu's death triggered PF fragmentation and a prolonged, still-unresolved burial dispute (courts ordered repatriation to Zambia Aug 2025; family granted appeal Dec 2025).
Brian Mundubile is the main opposition challenger as the Tonse–PF 'Pamodzi' Alliance candidate, running with Makebi Zulu; elected Tonse candidate 29 Jan 2026, filed nomination 19 May 2026.
Fred M'membe (Socialist Party, running mate Dolika Banda) runs separately; the broader 'United Opposition Front' collapsed over failure to agree a single flag-bearer.
26 presidential candidates registered; 14 approved by the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ).

Constitution (Amendment) Act No. 7 of 2025 ('Bill 7') passed 15 Dec 2025 and was assented 18 Dec 2025; it expanded the National Assembly and added 40 reserved/PR seats for women, youth, and persons with disabilities. An earlier version was struck down by the ConCourt on 27 June 2025.
A delimitation exercise added roughly 70 constituencies; critics allege gerrymandering favoring UPND strongholds (e.g., Southern Province 20→29 seats).

Approximately 8.7 million voters are registered; 758,000 new voters registered in late 2025; 86% of Zambians (Afrobarometer) say elections are the best way to choose leaders, implying high expected turnout.
Hichilema won the 2021 election with 59%, powered by strong youth turnout; youth remain a decisive bloc.
Zambia requires 50%+1 to win the presidency outright; a fragmented opposition splitting the anti-UPND vote structurally favors the incumbent.
Unscientific March 2026 Mwamba Facebook online poll (~40,800 votes): Hichilema ~52%, Mundubile ~27%, Makebi Zulu ~18%, Harry Kalaba ~4%.

Mundubile has shown unexpected campaign momentum via former PF networks across Copperbelt, Central, Eastern and Muchinga provinces, but faces resource/logistical disadvantages (reportedly denied air transport while HH campaigns freely).
Voter-priority issues are cost of living, unemployment and electricity (72% reported no/irregular power in 2024), giving the opposition an economic-grievance opening.

Analysts (ISS, Mail & Guardian) warn the election risks being seen as shaped by legal/constitutional manoeuvring rather than the ballot; M'membe was arrested under the 2025 Cyber Crimes Act in March 2026.
